description: Synthetic benchmark for evaluating context compression strategies. 8 tasks with mixed dependency relevance.

tasks.name: Database schema
tasks.prompt:
Create src/db.js with a SQLite database using better-sqlite3.

Define two tables:
- users: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY), email (TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL), password_hash (TEXT NOT NULL), created_at (TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
- tasks: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY), user_id (INTEGER REFERENCES users), title (TEXT NOT NULL), description (TEXT), status (TEXT DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK(status IN ('pending','in_progress','done'))), created_at (TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

Export the database instance and a function to initialize the tables.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: User authentication
tasks.prompt:
Create src/routes/auth.js with user registration and login endpoints.

POST /api/auth/register — accepts {email, password}, hashes password with bcryptjs, inserts into users table, returns {id, email}.
POST /api/auth/login — accepts {email, password}, verifies credentials, returns {token} (JWT signed with process.env.JWT_SECRET or 'dev-secret').

Mount the router in src/index.js.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Task CRUD endpoints
tasks.prompt:
Create src/routes/tasks.js with CRUD endpoints for the tasks table.

GET /api/tasks — list all tasks for the authenticated user.
POST /api/tasks — create a task {title, description}.
PUT /api/tasks/:id — update task {title, description, status}.
DELETE /api/tasks/:id — delete a task.

For now, do NOT add auth middleware — just accept a user_id query param for testing. Auth will be added later.

Mount the router in src/index.js.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Input validation middleware
tasks.prompt:
Create src/middleware/validate.js with request validation middleware.

Export functions:
- validateTask(req, res, next) — validates POST/PUT /api/tasks body. Title required and non-empty string. Status must be one of: pending, in_progress, done. Description optional.
- validateAuth(req, res, next) — validates POST /api/auth body. Email must be valid format. Password must be at least 8 characters.

Return 400 with {error: 'description'} on validation failure.

Apply validateTask to task routes and validateAuth to auth routes in their respective route files.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Auth middleware for task routes
tasks.prompt:
Create src/middleware/auth.js with JWT authentication middleware.

Export authenticateToken(req, res, next):
- Reads Authorization header (Bearer <token>)
- Verifies JWT with process.env.JWT_SECRET or 'dev-secret'
- Sets req.userId from token payload
- Returns 401 if missing/invalid token

Apply this middleware to all /api/tasks routes so they require authentication.
Update the task routes to use req.userId instead of the user_id query param.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Auth unit tests
tasks.prompt:
Create src/routes/auth.test.js with tests for the authentication endpoints using Node's built-in test runner.

Test:
- POST /api/auth/register with valid data returns 201 and user object
- POST /api/auth/register with duplicate email returns 409
- POST /api/auth/register with invalid email returns 400
- POST /api/auth/login with correct credentials returns token
- POST /api/auth/login with wrong password returns 401
- POST /api/auth/login with non-existent email returns 401

Use a test database (separate from main) to avoid data pollution.

Run the tests and make sure they pass.

tasks.name: Task CRUD unit tests
tasks.prompt:
Create src/routes/tasks.test.js with tests for the task CRUD endpoints using Node's built-in test runner.

Test:
- GET /api/tasks returns only tasks belonging to the authenticated user
- POST /api/tasks creates a task and returns 201
- POST /api/tasks without title returns 400 (validation)
- PUT /api/tasks/:id updates the task
- PUT /api/tasks/:id with invalid status returns 400
- DELETE /api/tasks/:id removes the task
- All endpoints return 401 without auth token

Register a test user and get a token before running task tests.
Use a test database to avoid data pollution.

Run the tests and make sure they pass.

tasks.name: API documentation
tasks.prompt:
Create API.md with comprehensive API documentation for the task management API.

Document every endpoint:
- Method, path, description
- Request body (with example JSON)
- Response body (with example JSON)
- Authentication requirements
- Possible error codes and messages

Also include:
- How to set up the project (npm install, environment variables)
- How to run the server
- How to run tests

Read all source files to ensure documentation matches the actual implementation.
